blocks.content: Flu rummaged through the supply box again and pulled out a heavy drone with both hands.
The drone, which flew using magnetic levitation, had erratic flight paths, making it suitable for guidance experiments.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let's start with level 1. Get a feel for it.
Shirone aimed the laser pointer at the drone.
When Flu flipped the switch, the drone vibrated and lifted off into the air.
The laser pointer tracked the drone's path.
Then, all eight Photon Cannons shot out simultaneously.
Though slower than the original Photon Cannon, the speed of the photonic magic was still impressive.
Shirone anxiously watched the homing Photon Cannons.
As the drone skimmed past the wall, the eight Photon Cannons curved sharply and struck the target.
Boom! Boom! Boom!

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Wow! That's amazing!
The precision of chasing the drone even after it bounced off. Plus, the power was strong enough to dent solid metal.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Magic... has no limits.
Finally realizing the reality, Shirone looked at Flu with eyes full of emotion.
Their gazes met for a moment before they ran to each other, hugging and jumping up and down.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Success! We did it, senpai!

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Well done! If you had failed, it would have been so frustrating! Finally, I'm free!
Their excited shouts echoed through the training ground.
Only the dented drone looked at them pitifully.
The last night at the Magic Association arrived.
Shirone headed to the first-floor waiting room after being called by Flu.
The empty hallway felt lonely compared to the bustling atmosphere of the day.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why the waiting room? We could have met at the dorm.
At the end of the hallway behind the first-floor lobby, light seeped through the crack of a door.
The darkness was silent, and no voices could be heard.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hello?
As Shirone opened the door and peeked in, a paper popper went off with a bang.
Startled, he stepped back, and the door opened to reveal four smiling faces.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Congratulations, Shirone!

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: Huh?
A cake was set up in the middle of the waiting room, with Flu, Gando, Isabel, and Kangnan standing around the table.
Flu lit the candles with a snap of her fingers and gestured for him to come in.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What are you doing? Come blow out the candles.
Feeling like he was in a dream, Shirone walked over.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Um, what's the occasion?

blocks.speaker: Isabel
blocks.content: It's the day you complete your internship at the Magic Association. Come on, hurry up.
When Shirone blew out the candles, everyone clapped.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thank you. But it's just an internship, nothing special.

blocks.speaker: Isabel
blocks.content: Still, it's the Magic Association. We can't let the longest-serving intern leave without a send-off. I heard you finished your training well. Flu said you were impressive. Congratulations.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: And you turned nineteen. Think of it as a birthday celebration.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh, right!
He had been so focused on training that he hadn't realized the year had changed.
The many events of the past year came back to him in a wave of emotions, much larger than anything he had felt before.
Standing a bit apart, Kangnan handed a small box over Isabel's shoulder.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Congratulations, Shirone. This is a gift from the association.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thank you. But what is it?
While he had formed connections with the people here, there was no reason for the association to care about an intern.
Sensing Gaold's influence, Shirone wondered if there was a hidden agenda.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: It's just a gift. Open it.
Inside the box was a silver signet ring. About 3 centimeters wide, with an engraved magic circle in the center.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh? This is a Kubrick, isn't it?

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: A Kubrick? A two-dimensional ring. All association staff have one. You know what a two-dimensional ring is, right?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, I don't.
Despite his rapid growth, Shirone knew little about magical tools.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: It's a ring that stores items using scale magic. They sell them at alchemy shops, but they're quite expensive. The ones from the association have a more precise encryption system, so they're pretty good.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A ring that stores items in a subspace would naturally be expensive.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: But what Shirone liked most was the Tormia Magic Association emblem engraved inside the ring.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No matter how expensive a Kubrick was, the association's emblem couldn't be bought with money.
Isabel took the ring and placed it on Shirone's ring finger.

blocks.speaker: Isabel
blocks.content: It works like other magical tools. It responds to the Spirit Zone.
Flu, could you handle the magic control device?
Flu went out to the hallway and disabled the magic control device for the waiting room.
Since the head of the magic library requested it, the security chief approved it, and the chief secretary allowed it, there was no problem.
When Flu returned, Shirone entered the Spirit Zone.
A unique magic he hadn't felt before was conveyed through shared perception.
Focusing on it, letters appeared before his eyes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: User registration? Yes, no?
As Shirone looked around for help, everyone tried to suppress their laughter.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Can you see the letters? They're visible only to you. They appear on your retina through brainwaves.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It must have used similar technology to project the view from the sky onto her retina.
After registration, a circle divided into seven slots like a pie chart appeared.
The slots were all the same size, and the top slot's border glowed red.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is this where items are stored?

blocks.speaker: Isabel
blocks.content: Yes. You can store up to 45 kilograms based on this world's gravity. If you fill one slot with 45 kilograms, you can't store anything in the remaining six slots.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wow, 45 kilograms?
That was enough to carry a decent military load. Plus, it would be incredibly convenient for long-distance travel, reducing the weight of food supplies.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let's practice. Choose an item to store first.
Shirone took out Armand from his waist.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Okay, then this...

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Alright. I'll teach you how. It's easy, like the four-directional technique.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Though he chuckled at the idea of the four-directional technique being easy, he accepted it as the standard of a third-class archmage.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: A Kubrick distinguishes objects through recognition within the mind. It's the core concept of external regulation. Since you recognize the sword as a complete item, the Kubrick separates it from the world. So you can't store just part of the sword.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Because I don't recognize it as complete.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Correct. Next is how to store and retrieve items. It's simple.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Touch the ring to the item and think about storing it. When your thought changes, the nature of the Spirit Zone changes slightly, and the Kubrick detects this subtle difference to operate. That's why living things can't be stored. Even the mind of a small creature changes the Spirit Zone, making it a foreign substance to the Kubrick.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone nodded repeatedly, impressed by the Kubrick's sensitivity.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Alright, I'll give it a try.
As he thought about storing Armand, the sword disappeared like magic.
He could see the now two-dimensional Armand stored in the Kubrick's slot.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Now to retrieve it.
When Armand appeared in front of his palm, he instinctively grabbed the handle.
Having fun, Shirone experimented with the function about a dozen times.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Once he got the hang of it, he understood the item well.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: If you go to a large enough alchemy shop in the city, you can upgrade the weight capacity. Up to 100 kilograms costs about 200 gold per kilogram. Beyond that, the price increases significantly, and if you exceed 400 kilograms, it can cost millions.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wow, that's incredible.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: The price was high, but so was the weight capacity. With 400 kilograms, you could carry all your belongings on your finger.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That means there's a high demand. It's also technically challenging to enhance. The current weight limit is around 800 kilograms. There might be one in the entire kingdom.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone was simply pleased. Whether it was 800 kilograms or not, he loved the ring he was wearing.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: No matter how convenient it is, it's best not to store important items unless you're traveling.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why is that? Could the items disappear...?

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: No. They could be stolen. The association-made Kubrick has a complex encryption system, so the risk of extraction is low. But anyone can cut off your finger and take it.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone instinctively covered the ring with his hand.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll protect it.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: There's no need for that. If you're in danger, just throw it away or give it up. Kubricks aren't unique, and you can buy them on the market.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This was advice only the second-in-command of the Magic Association could give.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Anyway, it's that kind of item. To become a proper mage, you need to know how to use it.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If he were to go to Heaven, a Kubrick would be essential. It was an unspoken pressure to be fully prepared, from important items to trivial ones.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, I'll definitely become a mage.
Shirone raised his hand, showing the Kubrick. Everyone smiled through their fingers.
